g.text: Hessian Approximation: Why It Matters for Quantization

div.p: Loss Landscape (Contour Plot) Contours show constant loss values. Closely spaced = steep curvature. Widely spaced = flat curvature.
Key Insight: Hessian tells us which weights to adjust and how much to minimize the output error caused by quantization.
Full Hessian: O(n²) storage For 4096 weights → 16M elements! Diagonal Approximation: O(n) H_diag = 2 · Σ(X²) per column
Fast to compute from calibration data. GPTQ reconstructs interaction info through column-by-column updates.
Result: INT4 quantization with < 1% perplexity increase vs. naive INT4 with 40%+ perplexity increase.

ul.li: H_ii large: steep direction Small weight change = large loss increase

H_ii small: flat direction
Can move freely with low cost
How changing w_i affects the optimal value of w_j
